titleStmt.title: Letter from Desmond FitzGerald to Mabel FitzGerald, 8 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Desmond FitzGerald

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from politician and intellectual Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947) to his wife Mabel FitzGerald (née Washington McConnell) (1884-1958) assuring her that he is well in prison. He inquires about news of the family and discusses the arrangements of his proposed release from prison in March 1916.This letter was written while Desmond FitzGerald was serving a six month sentence in Mountjoy prison having been convicted under the new Defence of the Realm legislation for making a seditious speech. He later served in the GPO during the Easter Rising, being placed in charge of the garrison's food supply. He initially escaped arrest after the Rising but was soon rounded up and imprisoned in Mountjoy, Dartmoor, Maidstone, Lewes and Portland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
M.D.M I am in excellent form.
Don't worry about me in the least.
I heard that you were going
to visit a gentleman of
Dr Browne's (Dolly's friend)
profession. Dowhenyou
wantnews. If my mother
Kate & F come over write
to ask that all 4 be allowed
together & that the time be
extended. You didn't lo
look at all well. Take care
of yourself. Go to every-
thing. Send me a card of
bachelor's buttons & I'll know
you got this all right. Nancy
shd be seeing one of her friends
on Dec. 7 th Go with her if
you can. And give her news
when she of is going on her
corporal works of mercy.
Go and see McCabe in A. Hill.
You knew him at the camp.
Cheer him up. I have seen
his friend here. Denis
presents to me I found the
following Tuesday & it
was a tremendous blessing
to me. I hope all went well
at the convention and that
your catering was a success
in the evening. Give me all
news news in detail in your
letter. Keep a diary & send
it in & complete other letters
in it. Hope all goes well in
B. & Kerry. and that my dear ones
are all well.
Get MacCarthy or someone
to clean & vaseline my
bike & that other plaything
in Meath Rd. Have Dolly
to stay with you as often as
she can. I know she would
cheer you up. It must
be dreadfully lonely down
there where you know no
one. I hope everyone is
very nice to you. I am
dreadfully fond of my dear
one all the time here. I am
due out last day of March
Friday in Lent. But it is
sweet things I crave for most
& dream about them everynight.
Remember what I said in Irish.
There might be a chance
next time. I hope everyone
agreed whatever
was best, Lovingly D.
